Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to calculate the total lead time for each case Lead time is from the first Submit Claim to the last Close Claim timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets confirm each again to make sure I did not mix up

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So first part identify long cases The short ones are 2001 and 2004 The long ones are 2002 2003 2005

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Low complexity cases are 2001 and 2004 both super fast no extra docs Medium is 2002 with 1 extra doc High are 2003 and 2005 with 2 and 3 extra docs

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So clearly higher complexity correlates with longer lead times because they require more additional document requests

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region A has 2001 fast and 2003 high with 48h Region B has 2002 at 26h 2004 fast and 2005 at 77h

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key difference between 2003 and 2005 both High is the time between additional document requests 2003s are 6h apart while 2005s are 29h30m and 22h apart

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Maybe Adjuster Lisa is overloaded so she cannot process the doc requests as quickly leading to longer waits between requests

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe Region B has stricter documentation requirements for high complexity claims leading to more required document requests

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Managers and Finance do not seem to be the bottleneck Manager Bill approval times after last document are similar and Finance is mostly same day

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Count the number of Request Additional Documents events per case 0 for 2001 and 2004 1 for 2002 2 for 2003 3 for 2005

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So number of doc requests is strongly correlated with lead time

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The initial evaluation time is fast for all cases so that is not a bottleneck The bottleneck is the document request cycle

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The delays between request events are partly customer response time plus the time the adjuster takes to review them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity is the primary driver of longer lead times Low complexity cases complete in under 2 hours medium takes about 26 hours and high takes 48 to 77 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource performance is tied to claim complexity and regional workload Adjusters handling high complexity claims in overloaded regions like Adjuster Lisa in Region B are more likely to cause delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Implement a pre submission checklist for high complexity claims Assign dedicated senior adjusters to high complexity claims Balance adjuster workloads across regions Standardize follow up processes and provide training for thorough initial evaluations
